commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7cc9d5d3c6c8ee3969048ae8801206ea4e316810)
commands.review_summary:
The branch imports OpenRouter model-id normalization into `normalizeResolvedModel`, rewrites resolved `model.id` values with an `openrouter/` prefix, and adds unit assertions for Hunter Alpha and Anthropic refs.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +18. Total +21 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main sends the resolved OpenRouter `model.id` through the OpenAI-compatible payload unchanged, and the PR-introduced regression is source-reproducible because its helper strips `openrouter/auto` to `auto`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor or maintainer revision because the patch can break the existing OpenRouter auto default and the proof is unit-only.

target.title: fix(openrouter): strip openrouter/ prefix from model ID in normalizeResolvedModel hook (fixes #92611)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92611 — OpenRouter Anthropic models (and any model with `openrouter/` prefix) send the wrong model ID to the API, causing HTTP 400 errors.

## Root Cause

`normalizeOpenRouterResolvedModel()` in `extensions/openrouter/index.ts` normalizes `baseUrl` and `reasoning` but does **not** normalize `model.id`. When OpenClaw registers an OpenRouter model with ID `openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6`, that full string is sent to the OpenRouter API, which expects `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6`.

## Fix

Uses the existing `normalizeOpenRouterModelId()` from `./models.js` (which already strips the `openrouter/` prefix) in the `normalizeOpenRouterResolvedModel` hook. Also adds model ID to the early-return check so models without the prefix pass through unchanged.

**Changed files:**
- `extensions/openrouter/index.ts` — +4 lines (import + normalize ID in hook)
- `extensions/openrouter/index.test.ts` — +18 lines (assertion for existing test + new Anthropic model test case)

## Testing

All 35 OpenRouter provider tests pass, including:
- Existing `normalizedGptModel` test (no `openrouter/` prefix → unchanged)
- Existing `normalizedHunterModel` test (has `openrouter/` prefix → now also asserts ID stripped)
- New `normalizedAnthropicModel` test (verifies `openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6` → `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6`)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** OpenRouter model ID normalization strips `openrouter/` prefix before sending to API
- **Environment tested:** macOS, Node 22, pnpm workspace
- **Steps run after the patch:**
  1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/openrouter/index.test.ts`
  2. Verified `normalizeOpenRouterModelId("openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6")` returns `"anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6"`
- **Evidence after fix:**

```
✓  extension-providers  extensions/openrouter/index.test.ts (35 tests) 55ms
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  35 passed (35)
```

- **Observed result after fix:** All 35 tests pass. Model ID `openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6` is correctly normalized to `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6`.
- **What was not tested:** Live OpenRouter API call (requires API key); the normalization logic is verified through unit tests.
target.author: liuhao1024
